summary: Draft candidate package for PerfCalculateActualUtilization under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power. The clean Win25H2Clean baseline confirmed PerfCalculateActualUtilization=1, the repo power notes carry an exact docs hit, the shared string batch found an exact current-build ntoskrnl.exe hit, the shared Ghidra batch produced reviewable xref artifacts, and the shared clean-baseline Procmon follow-up stayed shell-safe but did not materialize usable per-candidate output files. The current app does not ship this raw value as a standalone supported tweak surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Docs/power/power.md:181 shows `PerfCalculateActualUtilization` with observed literal `1` in the repo power notes.
validation_proof.notes: The docs-first triage and phase-0 baseline agree on `PerfCalculateActualUtilization=1` for the current Win25H2Clean baseline. The value remains research-only because the app does not currently ship this raw registry control as a standalone supported surface.

setting.name: Perf Calculate Actual Utilization
setting.casual_explanation: Perf Calculate Actual Utilization is a low-level power-manager registry value under the main Power key.
setting.why_people_change_it: Researchers track this value when reviewing processor performance accounting and utilization heuristics.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: This value appears in low-level processor performance logic, but the shipped app now prefers the documented PERFBOOSTMODE surface instead of this raw registry path.
setting.professional_notes: Win25H2Clean phase-0 existence confirmed PerfCalculateActualUtilization on the live baseline with preview value 1. The docs-first triage then tied the value to exact repo documentation hits, led by Docs/power/power.md:181. A shared current-build string batch found an exact hit in C:\Windows\System32\ntoskrnl.exe, and the shared Ghidra batch on the same binary preserved a reviewable xref/decompile artifact set for the docs-first power-control family. A shared Procmon-safe runtime batch was then rerun from RegProbe-Baseline-Clean-20260329, but all seven values came back copy-incomplete with no guest-side wrapper-error file copied back. That keeps the runtime lane honest but incomplete.

allowed_values.label: Observed Win25H2Clean baseline
allowed_values.meaning: The current clean VM baseline exposes PerfCalculateActualUtilization=1.

targets.notes: This candidate is tracked as a raw kernel-facing value, not as a published end-user tweak profile.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app uses the documented PERFBOOSTMODE command surface and does not ship PerfCalculateActualUtilization as a standalone raw registry value.

windows_defaults.label: Observed Win25H2Clean baseline

states.rationale: Keep the currently observed baseline value (1) while this raw control stays in research packaging only.

windows_defaults.notes: Observed directly from the 96-key phase-0 existence batch on Win25H2Clean.

recommended_profiles.label: Observed baseline

states.rationale: Track the observed baseline value without turning it into a shipped preset.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the repo to track the live baseline and current evidence for this raw power-manager value.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a supported end-user tweak or a documented Microsoft control surface.
tradeoffs: Keeps the candidate research-only and non-actionable.

evidence.title: Repo power notes for docs-first power-control values
evidence.summary: Repo power notes contain an exact entry for PerfCalculateActualUtilization, led by Docs/power/power.md:181.

evidence.title: Win25H2Clean 96-key phase-0 existence batch
evidence.summary: The 96-key existence batch confirmed PerfCalculateActualUtilization exists on the clean baseline with observed value 1.

evidence.title: Shared docs-first string triage for current-build ntoskrnl
evidence.summary: Shared string triage found an exact current-build ntoskrnl.exe string hit for PerfCalculateActualUtilization.

evidence.title: Shared Ghidra xref batch for docs-first power-control values
evidence.summary: The shared ntoskrnl Ghidra batch produced a reviewable string/xref artifact set for PerfCalculateActualUtilization; some values resolved naturally while several remained forced-boundary fallback blocks.

evidence.title: Shared clean-baseline Procmon follow-up for docs-first power-control values
evidence.summary: The shared Procmon-safe runtime follow-up stayed shell-safe on RegProbe-Baseline-Clean-20260329 but returned copy-incomplete for PerfCalculateActualUtilization, leaving no usable per-candidate guest capture file.

decision.why: PerfCalculateActualUtilization is now packaged as a real candidate with live baseline existence, exact repo-doc hits, shared ntoskrnl string and Ghidra corroboration, and a clean-baseline runtime follow-up. It still stays research-gated because the app does not ship this raw registry value as a standalone supported surface and the shared runtime lane did not materialize usable exact-read artifacts.
blocking_issues: No shipped standalone app mapping for the raw registry value.
Shared clean-baseline runtime capture stayed copy-incomplete and did not yield per-candidate guest artifacts.
